focus: traceability: handlers README claims against merged handler implementations

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: One README makes behavioral claims across four non-trivial handler implementations.

targetSelection.selectionReason: The slice plan names the README and all four implementations needed to adjudicate its handler-level claims.

searchLedger.invariant: The README's scan ownership statement must be backed by coalescing plus full-scan orphan and suspect maintenance.

searchActions.queryOrPath: README scan and orphan-sweep claims; memory-index scan lease and maintenance phases
searchActions.result: Coalescing is returned for active/recent leases; unscoped scans run suspect confirmation and global orphan sweep.

searchLedger.rationale: The behavior and stated ownership agree; scoped repairs intentionally skip global maintenance without contradicting the ownership-level README claim.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct implementation evidence supports the claim.

searchLedger.invariant: When enabled, query-time filtering must occur before formatting and preserve missing-path IDs for later drift handling.

searchActions.queryOrPath: README filter claim; flag gate, filter helper, and pipeline integration
searchActions.result: The filter is flag-gated, excludes absent paths, records IDs, and the cache is bypassed while active.

searchLedger.rationale: The implementation realizes the described opt-in filtering and drift-suspect handoff.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct implementation evidence supports the claim.

searchLedger.invariant: The decomposed save pipeline must serialize same-folder saves through the documented handler boundary.

searchActions.result: memory-save invokes the mutex; the mutex composes an in-process promise chain with an interprocess lock and releases it in finally.

searchLedger.rationale: The save orchestration documentation is consistent with the concrete concurrency boundary.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Exact usage and implementation evidence support the claim.

searchLedger.invariant: The README's context-mode statement must map to configured modes and concrete strategy execution.

searchActions.result: auto, quick, deep, focused, and resume modes are configured; deep, focused, and resume executors are implemented and the handler routes requests after validation.

searchLedger.rationale: The documented context responsibility is supported by the handler configuration and execution paths.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct implementation evidence supports the claim.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The merged handler layout may leave a README claim that no longer maps to an exported handler surface.

searchActions.result: The lazy registry exports both reviewed public handlers; the README's layer and ownership descriptions remain aligned.

searchLedger.rationale: No stale or unsupported substantive README claim was found in the reviewed slice.

focus: maintainability: system-spec-kit skill-doc alignment for drift-marker and generated-metadata pruning surfaces

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Two operating documents and one cross-skill integration surface define maintenance and destructive pruning behavior.

targetSelection.selectionReason: The iteration plan names the system-spec-kit skill, changed references, the scripts pruning contract, and deep-loop surfaces that cite their behavior.
omittedHighRiskTargets: No implementation mutation target was reviewed; the iteration was restricted to documentation-alignment.

searchLedger.invariant: The skill must direct script users to a current, bounded script architecture authority.

searchActions.result: SKILL.md names scripts/README.md as the script architecture authority and the README identifies current entrypoints and source boundaries.

searchLedger.rationale: The documents form an intentional authority chain without a contradictory ownership claim.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct-read comparison found no unsupported or conflicting contract.

searchLedger.invariant: Destructive generated-metadata pruning must preserve report-before-apply confirmation, scope binding, stale-report refusal, and recovery guidance.

searchActions.queryOrPath: scripts README generated metadata pruning section
searchActions.result: The contract requires --prune-report followed by --prune with the reviewed contentHash under identical scope controls, refuses stale reports, and specifies backup and restore behavior.

searchLedger.rationale: The operator contract exposes the full safety boundary rather than a shorthand destructive command.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: All required safeguards are explicitly documented.

searchLedger.invariant: The deep-review integration surface must cite the canonical Spec Kit resolver and ownership boundary rather than a retired local path.

searchActions.queryOrPath: system-spec-kit references in system-deep-loop deep-review surface
searchActions.result: The deep-review skill cites resolveArtifactRoot from the system-spec-kit shared module and names system-spec-kit as the command-owned state and continuity owner.

searchLedger.rationale: The cross-skill reference points to the current shared seam and preserves the ownership distinction.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No stale local resolver or conflicting owner claim was found.

focus: traceability: ENV_REFERENCE.md flag defaults and runtime reads

findingDetails.title: Archived vector inclusion has conflicting documented defaults
findingDetails.claim: The reference declares SPECKIT_INCLUDE_ARCHIVED_VECTOR graduated ON while the startup path describes it as opt-in/default-off.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked orphan-sweep budget, enforcement-default, evaluation logging, and consumption-log contracts for equivalent default mismatches.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The startup comment rather than the reference row may be stale; either way the unset-default contract conflicts.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Direct flag-resolver evaluation demonstrates default-on and the startup comment is corrected as stale in the same change.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search located the conflicting SPECKIT_INCLUDE_ARCHIVED_VECTOR references; no class-wide assertion is made.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile the reference row and startup-path contract against the flag evaluator, then retain one explicit unset-default statement.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: A central environment reference makes behaviorally significant default and enforcement claims across multiple runtime and script consumers.

targetSelection.selectionReason: The configured slice names the environment reference and specifically calls out orphan-sweep, enforcement, consumption-log, calibration, archived-row, and graduation contracts.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The archived-vector row may document a different default than the runtime startup path.

searchActions.result: Reference says graduated ON; startup path says opt-in/default-off.

searchLedger.rationale: The contradictory unset-default statements make the operator contract untrustworthy.

searchLedger.invariant: The documented defaults and clamp behavior match the runtime configuration reads.

searchActions.result: Runtime reads both variables and records clamp warnings.

searchLedger.rationale: No contradictory default or control-path claim was found.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Exact read sites agree with the reference contract.

searchLedger.invariant: Graduated enforcement defaults and opt-in consumption logging have a matching consumer read.

searchActions.result: Reviewed enforcement scripts default true and consumption logging requires an explicit enabling value.

searchLedger.rationale: The reviewed default semantics agree with their implementation reads.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No second default contradiction was evidenced.
